def solve(nums)
    # Helper function to check if a number is prime
    def is_prime(n)
        if n <= 1
            return false
        end
        if n <= 3
            return true # 2 and 3 are prime
        end
        if n % 2 == 0 || n % 3 == 0
            return false
        end
        i = 5
        while i * i <= n
            if n % i == 0 || n % (i + 2) == 0
                return false
            end
            i += 6
        end
        return true
    end

    total_operations = 0
    n = nums.length

    for i in 0...n
        current_val = nums[i]
        
        if i.even?
            # Even index: Must be prime
            target = current_val
            while !is_prime(target)
                target += 1
            end
            cost = target - current_val
            total_operations += cost
        else
            # Odd index: Must be non-prime (i.e., not prime)
            target = current_val
            # We stop when target is NOT prime. 
            # Since nums[i] >= 1, the target will be at least 1.
            while is_prime(target)
                target += 1
            end
            cost = target - current_val
            total_operations += cost
        end
    end
    
    return total_operations
end

def solve(nums)
    MAX_VAL = 100000 + 100 # Sufficiently large upper bound

    is_prime_sieve = Array.new(MAX_VAL + 1, true)
    is_prime_sieve[0] = is_prime_sieve[1] = false
    
    p = 2
    while p * p <= MAX_VAL
        if is_prime_sieve[p]
            (p * p..MAX_VAL).step(p) do i
                is_prime_sieve[i] = false
            end
        end
        p += 1
    end

    def check_prime(n, sieve)
        return false if n < 0 || n > sieve.length - 1
        sieve[n]
    end

    total_operations = 0
    
    # 2. Iterate through the array
    nums.each_with_index do |n, i|
        current_cost = 0
        
        if i % 2 == 0 # Even index: Must be prime
            
            # Check if n is already prime
            if check_prime(n, is_prime_sieve)
                # Already prime, cost = 0
            else
                # Find smallest prime N' >= n
                target_n_prime = n
                while true
                    if target_n_prime > MAX_VAL
                        # This should ideally not happen given problem constraints
                        raise "Search exceeded MAX_VAL" 
                    end
                    if check_prime(target_n_prime, is_prime_sieve)
                        break
                    end
                    target_n_prime += 1
                end
                current_cost = target_n_prime - n
            end
            
        else # Odd index: Must be non-prime
            
            # Check if n is already non-prime
            # Non-prime includes 1 and composite numbers
            if n <= 1 || !check_prime(n, is_prime_sieve)
                # Already non-prime, cost = 0
            else
                # n is prime. Find smallest non-prime N' >= n
                target_n_prime = n
                while true
                    if target_n_prime > MAX_VAL
                        raise "Search exceeded MAX_VAL"
                    end
                    # Check if target_n_prime is NOT prime
                    is_target_non_prime = (target_n_prime <= 1) || !check_prime(target_n_prime, is_prime_sieve)
                    
                    if is_target_non_prime
                        break
                    end
                    target_n_prime += 1
                end
                current_cost = target_n_prime - n
            end
        end
        
        total_operations += current_cost
    end
    
    return total_operations
